stem: An engineer takes 10 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 138.9 % and a standard deviation of 11.2 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically overestimates the true value by between 3.7% and 6.5%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 138.9 %. After correcting for the 5.1% bias: ≈ 132.2 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (3.7%-6.5%) and sampling variability: [123.3, 141.0]. Point estimate: 132.2 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
